task_completion.description: Verify that a new folder named 'Critical-Thread' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a folder named 'Critical-Thread' in the email client's folder pane or sidebar.

task_completion.description: Verify that emails related to the critical Bug ID (4096) have been moved to the 'Critical-Thread' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of the 'Critical-Thread' folder. The message list should display emails referencing 'Bug 4096'.

task_completion.description: Verify that an email is being drafted to the correct recipient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the email compose window. The 'To' or recipient field must contain 'dev-team@company.com'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the drafted email has the correct subject line including the critical Bug ID.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Subject' field in the email compose window. It must exactly read 'Escalation: Bug 4096'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the drafted email body states the exact number of messages found.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main text area of the email compose window. It must contain the text 'Found 7 messages' (or a very similar statement explicitly mentioning the number 7).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a graphical email client to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show a recognizable GUI email application (like Thunderbird, Evolution, or a webmail interface), not just terminal windows manipulating maildir files.

integrity.description: Verify the email draft is created within the email application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draft should be visible in an actual email compose window, not written in a basic text editor like Notepad or Nano.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI folder creation and email moving.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'mkdir Critical-Thread' or 'mv *4096* Critical-Thread/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The critical Bug ID with the most messages is '4096'. There are exactly 7 messages related to this bug. The expected folder name is 'Critical-Thread'. The report recipient is 'dev-team@company.com'. The expected subject is 'Escalation: Bug 4096' and the expected body text should state 'Found 7 messages'.
